column1: example
I was trying to get her number but my rizz was nonexistent last night — ended up talking about my grandma's cats for 15 minutes.
Bro has so much rizz he could charm the Wi-Fi back on during a blackout.
His pickup line was so cringe, zero rizz detected — she just blinked twice and walked away.
I don't need rizz, I need a nap and maybe a will to live, but sure, watch him flirt his way into free guacamole.
No cap, I saw a squirrel ride a tiny skateboard across campus yesterday, and I need video evidence.
He said he could bench 300 pounds — huge cap, I saw him struggling with a gallon of milk.
This pizza is the best thing I've eaten all month, no cap, I might propose to it.
The movie was so mid I forgot the plot while I was still in the theater seat.
Her presentation wasn't bad, just mid, like a plain rice cake with no toppings.
That burger joint everyone hyped up was aggressively mid — the fries were cold before we even sat down.
She slayed that job interview in her thrifted blazer and unmatched confidence.
My mom cooked Thanksgiving dinner for 20 people and slayed every single dish.
You don't need to slay every day — sometimes just surviving is the ultimate slay.
She ate that performance and left no crumbs on the stage — the crowd is still recovering.
His apology was pathetic, but his outfit? Ate down, absolutely devoured the runway.
That taco truck ate so hard I'm going back tomorrow, consequences be damned.
You lose 500 aura points for texting your ex at 2 AM — straight to jail vibes.
He walked into the coffee shop like a final boss, his aura so loud it ordered for him.
Spilling your drink then catching it mid-air? Infinite aura gain unlocked.
He's aura farming by pretend-reading a philosophy book in the back corner of the cafeteria.
Posting your gym selfie with a caption about 'grinding' is just aura farming with extra steps.
She's delulu for thinking she can finish that 10-page essay in 20 minutes — but honestly, respect the delusion.
Me thinking my crush will confess at the school assembly even though we've never spoken: pure delulu.
Some people call it delusional, I call it manifestation with ✨ pizazz ✨.
He stared at the vending machine for five minutes like an NPC glitching on the sidewalk.
Her small talk was so NPC-coded I felt like I was in a generic video game tutorial.
Walking in straight lines and saying 'have a nice day' with zero eye contact — that's NPC energy if I've ever seen it.
It's giving ✨ desperate ✨ with how fast he double-texted after she left him on read.
Your outfit today is giving 'grandma's couch at 3 AM' and weirdly, I love it.
This lecture is giving 'sad beige classroom' — I need caffeine just to stay conscious.
I didn't study for the final and I saw the question sheet — I'm absolutely cooked.
Burnt the toast, missed the bus, and my phone died at 10% — today is so cooked it's not even funny.
He tried to fight the teacher's Wi-Fi password and lost — cooked beyond belief.
When the WiFi cut out during the last round of Valorant, my brother had a full crashout — keyboard flew.
She nearly crashed out after the printer ate her 20-page resume right before submission.
Three hours of sleep and four coffees in, I'm one minor inconvenience away from a silent crashout in the library.
He stayed completely silent for the entire class — not because he was listening, but because he was mewing for jawline gains.
I've been mewing for six months and the only change is my tongue hurts. Send help.
The guy in front of me in history class has his tongue glued to the roof of his mouth, mewing like his life depends on it.
I'm on my looksmaxxing arc — skincare, gym, and pretending I don't eat instant ramen at 2 AM.
He's been looksmaxxing so hard he replaced his whole wardrobe and started mewing in his sleep.
When she walked in with that prom dress, she absolutely mogged everyone else in the room.
His yearbook photo looks like a modeling portfolio — he's mogging the entire senior class without even trying.
I walked into the party feeling confident, then Chad walked in and I got mogged into next week.
He was yapping about his fantasy football team for an hour — I zoned out after the first 30 seconds.
My little sister won't stop yapping about Skibidi Toilet lore; I don't understand a single word.
The teacher kept yapping about the syllabus like we hadn't read it three times already.
Don't glaze him just because he can do a kickflip — he still owes me ten bucks.
She was glazing her new sneakers so hard, I thought they were about to shine directly into my soul.
I asked customer support for a human but got a clanker chatbot instead — immediately asked for a refund.
That AI-generated voice on the phone is such a clanker, I hung up out of sheer annoyance.
He called my outfit chopped, but he's wearing socks with sandals, so his opinion is invalid.
The new haircut is so chopped, he looks like a startled hedgehog.
I saw my doppelgänger today — but she was the chopped version, shorter, sadder, and wearing Crocs.
That actor's stunt double is his choppleganger — same face, but somehow made of disappointment.
He thought he looked like Zayn Malik, but everyone saw a choppleganger from Wish dot com.
Gyatt! That truck is massive — is that road legal or are we just ignoring physics?
She said 'gyatt' to a picture of a really fluffy cat, and honestly? Accurate.
Every time he walks past the gym mirrors, there's a chorus of 'gyattt' from his boys. He hates it.
That's so skibidi, I can't tell if it's genius or brain damage.
My little brother is obsessed with Skibidi Toilet — he won't stop making toilet sounds at dinner.
She described her weekend as 'skibidi sigma vibes' and I need Google Translate for Gen Z.
He sat alone at lunch, reading a book and ignoring everyone — total sigma behavior, or maybe just socially anxious.
Not going to the party because you're 'grinding' is either sigma dedication or a convenient excuse. We'll never know.
Waking up at 4 AM, cold shower, no friends, no fun — the sigma grindset sounds like a sad spreadsheet.
Asking for permission to speak in the group chat is beta behavior — just send the meme, coward.
He thinks he's an alpha for ordering for his date, but she's lactose intolerant and he ordered cheese pizza.
Based take: pineapple on pizza is actually delicious and you're all just followers.
My teacher said homework should be banned — based beyond belief, give her a raise.
His entire TikTok account is cringe — from the forced laughs to the weird transitions.
Watching your parents try to dab in public is peak cringe, but kind of adorable.
Got a free coffee because the barista messed up my name? Massive W for me and my terrible handwriting.
Taking the wrong bus to the wrong side of town for an exam you're already failing? That's an L of epic proportions.
He finally asked her out and she said yes — huge W for the underdog squad.
Spilling hot sauce on your white shirt before a presentation: big L energy.
You think you can beat me in Mario Kart? Bet. But I'm warning you, I'm petty with blue shells.
If you finish your homework before midnight, I'll do your chores. Bet?
He was laughing at his own joke before anyone else — kinda sus, not gonna lie.
The way she looked at the last slice of pizza was sus — like she was about to commit a crime.
This gas station sandwich has no right being this bussin', I'm shook.
Grandma's secret recipe cookies are absolutely bussin', I could eat the whole batch.
When he walked into the pole, I was dead — my soul left my body for a solid five seconds.
She texted 'dead' after the teacher tripped on the cord, and I felt that spiritually.
His outfit today is so drippy, he looks like a million bucks and a rainy cloud all at once.
New shoes, fresh hoodie, hat on backwards — the drip is immaculate today.
Your fit for the concert is giving 2000s boy band, and I'm here for the chaos.
I changed my fit three times before leaving — every option was a cry for help.
The debate between those two was a total mid-off — both were aggressively mediocre.
Two bland sandwiches competing for 'least sad lunch' — it's a mid-off of depressing proportions.
Nice opinions, bro. Now put the fries in the bag and focus on your shift.
He thinks he's an alpha but works a dead-end job — put the fries in the bag, buddy.
It's 11 PM, I have three assignments due tomorrow, and I'm spiraling into a YouTube rabbit hole about ant farms.
She saw her ex with someone new and started spiraling — three hours of sad music and frozen yogurt.
His school talent show performance was a hard watch — I felt secondhand embarrassment for his entire bloodline.
The movie had so many awkward pauses it became a hard watch by minute ten.
Her glow-up from freshman year to senior year is insane — new haircut, new confidence, new attitude.
My summer glow-up consisted of learning to cook eggs without setting off the fire alarm. Progress?
Me actually finishing my homework before midnight? Uncharted territory for my procrastinating soul.
The group chat was quiet for an hour — uncharted territory, I'm genuinely scared.
I've been scrolling TikTok for six hours and my brainrot is terminal — send memes.
He can't hold a conversation without quoting Skibidi Toilet — that's brainrot of the highest order.
Failed three tests and lost my favorite hoodie — I'm officially in my flop era, don't talk to me.
Started saying no to plans and prioritizing my peace — I'm in my villain era and I love it.
Bought a coffee, wore a trench coat, and ignored everyone at the mall — it's my main character era, don't interrupt.
He was out at 3 AM in the rain looking for a taco truck — straight on demon time, no regrets.
Me studying at 2 AM fueled by energy drinks and spite — on demon time, finals edition.
Sheesh, those dance moves are fire — did he rehearse in secret for years?
Sheesh, that homework load is criminal — did the teacher just assign war and peace?
He cried during a commercial and called himself a soyboy — honestly, emotional intelligence is hot.
Drinking oat milk and apologizing for existing — peak soyboy behavior, and I'm not mad at it.
He took the red pill and now won't shut up about conspiracy theories — send help.
Don't go down the red pill rabbit hole, it's just rage bait and terrible takes.
I choose the blue pill — ignorance is bliss and I don't need to know my future.
After that rejection, he's fully on the black pill and won't stop sighing dramatically.
The WiFi finally came back after an hour — we're so back, prepare for memes.
The pizza place forgot my extra cheese — it's so over, my day is ruined.
Found a $20 in my old jeans pocket — we're so back, lunch is on me.
You've been arguing about fictional characters for three hours — go touch grass, please.
I told my brother to touch grass after he tried to explain Skibidi Toilet lore for an hour.
You're upset about a downvote? Bro, go touch grass and remember the sun exists.
That embarrassing thing I said in 2019 lives rent-free in my head at 3 AM.
Her laugh lives rent-free in my brain — I can hear it when I brush my teeth.
The way he said 'you too' to the waiter lives rent-free in my cringe collection.
She walked into class late with coffee and sunglasses — main character energy all the way.
Me crying in the rain after a minor inconvenience — main character energy, but the sad indie film version.
I planned the group hangout and everyone's schedule is ruined — chaos coordinator, reporting for duty.
She organized the secret santa and somehow created three different alliances — absolute chaos coordinator.
I'm in my silent treatment era — not responding to texts, just vibing in the void.
My group chat is ignoring me for no reason — silent treatment era initiated, I guess.
He tweeted something dumb and got ratio'd so hard his mentions are a disaster zone.
When you ratio the ratio — mind blown, internet is undefeated.
Stop gatekeeping your playlist — share the songs, we're all sad here.
She's gatekeeping the name of her nail salon like it's national security.
He said he never ate the last slice of pizza, but I saw the crumbs — textbook gaslighting.
My phone says 8 AM but the sun says midnight — who's gaslighting me, nature or technology?
She negotiated a raise while doing her makeup — ultimate girlboss energy, I'm taking notes.
Girlbossing too close to the sun? Maybe, but she's doing it in heels.
He did the dishes, folded laundry, and had dinner ready when she got home — malewife material, lock him down.
My boyfriend is the ultimate malewife — he even irons my shirts without being asked.
He only drinks lukewarm water — beige flag, not red, not green, just… room temp.
She organizes her books by color, not genre — that's a beige flag but I'm intrigued.
He said he doesn't like dogs — biggest red flag I've ever seen, run for the hills.
They text their ex at midnight — red flag with neon lights, that's not a relationship, that's a hostage situation.
She brought me soup when I was sick without asking — green flag, marry her immediately.
He listens more than he talks — that's a green flag the size of Texas.
He chews with his mouth open — instant ick, I'm out, cancel the wedding.
She said 'we' in her apology — ick. Total ick. You're not the royal family.
They've been sneaky links for months but won't admit it — the group chat knows everything.
He came over at 2 AM and left before sunrise — sneaky link behavior, very sus.
We're not dating, but we're not not dating — it's a situationship and it's emotionally draining.
He sends good morning texts but won't define the relationship — textbook situationship hell.
We're in the talking stage — which means we send memes back and forth and I overanalyze every period.
The talking stage lasted two weeks until he sent a picture of his pet iguana. Then I knew.
I thought we were in love, but he didn't even know my last name — pure delusionship.
Our entire relationship was a delusionship invented by my brain at 2 AM.
He sends a text once a week just to keep me interested — breadcrumbing at its finest, no thanks.
She likes my stories but never replies to DMs — that's breadcrumbing and I'm too tired for it.
He watches all my stories but won't talk to me — orbiting in my DMs like a creepy satellite.
She's orbiting my social media but won't engage — just floating around my posts like a ghost.
He pops up every few months like an unwanted paperclip — paperclipping is emotional terrorism.
An ex just texted after a year of silence — classic paperclipping, delete and ignore.
She ghosted for six months then texted like nothing happened — zombieing is undead energy.
He appeared out of nowhere after a year — zombieing back from the dead, but I'm not a graveyard.
I'm not overwhelmed or underwhelmed — just whelmed. Exactly medium. Perfectly fine.
The movie was okay, the popcorn was okay, the seats were okay — I'm whelmed, and that's fine.
Quick question — why is the water bill $400 when I showered at the gym all month?
Quick question: who decided pineapples belong on pizza, and can I fight them?
I have a question — why is my phone on 2% and my charger cable is 'not compatible' when it literally came with the phone?
I have a question — why do we park in a driveway and drive on a parkway? Who named these things?
I'm not okay — I just watched a sad dog commercial and now I'm crying into my cereal.
She texted 'I'm not okay' after finals, so we brought snacks and trashy TV to her dorm.
I accidentally kicked over the trash can at the gas station — sorry for party rocking, I guess.
He spilled soda on the teacher's desk and whispered 'sorry for party rocking' — detention speedrun.
Woke up, brushed my teeth, and decided to slay the day away — but then I took a nap for four hours.
She plans to slay the day away with errands and iced coffee — the ultimate adulting combo.
She's a 10 but she texts 'k' with a period after it — instant drop to a 3.
He's a 10 but his personality is just TikTok sounds — hard pass.
I'm a 10 but I cry when I see a really cute dog — still a 10, just emotionally available.
Q: What time is it? A: Six-seven. Time doesn't matter in brainrot world.
He answered every question in class with '6-7' and somehow the teacher just moved on.
She hit me with the '6-7' hand sign in the hallway and I felt legally required to nod back.
She said my cooking 'ate' and I just replied 'Onika burgers' — she looked so confused.
When someone overpraises a mediocre meal, just whisper 'Onika burgers' and walk away.
He told me to 'start digging in your butt, twin' and I genuinely have no response.
She said that phrase to the teacher as a joke and the silence was deafening — we all stared at the floor.
A guy riding a unicycle while playing the flute in traffic? Only in Ohio, man.
The school mascot fought a parking cone — only in Ohio, I can't explain it.
The room looked like the Grimace shake incident after the party — purple stains everywhere.
He drank a purple smoothie and pretended to pass out — we're still doing Grimace shake jokes, apparently.
I took a bite of my sister's sandwich and yelled 'Fanum tax' — she was not amused.
He tried to charge a Fanum tax on my birthday cake, so I charged him emotional damage fees.
He ate three glizzies at the baseball game and didn't even flinch — I'm disturbed and impressed.
Glizzy gobbler contest at the county fair? I'll pass, but I'll watch from a safe distance.
The kid with the baseball cap and shorts in freezing weather? Total Mason.
He keeps saying '6-7' and touching his face — he's a full-on Mason, there's no saving him.
He thinks he's the next big athlete — total Baby Gronk energy, all hype no stats.
My little cousin acts like Baby Gronk at family gatherings — he's 7 and already too much.
He's trying so hard to impress a Livvy Dunne type — bro, you're not in her league, chill.
Every guy in the gym thinks he's dating Livvy Dunne in his head — delulu central.
Bro thinks he has Duke Dennis level charisma — he really just stood there and blinked.
She said my vibe was 'Duke Dennis energy' and I had to Google it — still not sure if that's a compliment.
Why is he making that goofy ahh face in every selfie — unhinged.
His dance moves are so goofy ahh, but he commits to the bit, so respect.
He said 'ope, just gonna sneak past ya' — pure Midwest energy in its natural habitat.
Putting ranch on everything and apologizing for no reason — that's the Midwest energy I grew up with.
She walked into class, looked at the teacher, and said 'what's good?' — east coast energy, no filter.
He almost got into a fight over a parking spot — classic east coast energy, I had popcorn ready.
She showed up to the meeting late with a smoothie and sunglasses — west coast energy is unmatched.
His entire vibe is 'chill, dude' — west coast energy, even though he's never left Ohio.
Wearing turquoise and talking about the desert sunset — southwest vibes, very aesthetic.
He tried to manifest a vacation to Arizona — southwest vibes only in his dreams, for now.
She showed up in flannel, talking about hiking and rain — pure Pacific Northwest vibes.
The coffee shop had exposed brick and sad indie music — peak Pacific Northwest vibes.
His idea of a fun Friday night is organizing his books by height — unhinged in the most harmless way.
She laughed at her own joke for five minutes — unhinged but honestly, goals.
He wakes up at 4 AM to run — deranged behavior, but his endurance is impressive.
She drank coffee at 10 PM to prepare for an all-nighter — deranged, but I respect the hustle.
I have three alarms set, a backup charger, and snacks — I'm prepared for war, aka Monday morning.
She packed two outfits, makeup, and snacks for a three-hour class — prepared for war against boredom.
I nodded along to the teacher but my brain was on a beach somewhere — mentally checked out by 9 AM.
He stared at the clock for an hour — mentally checked out, his soul is already on summer break.
I raised my hand in class and forgot what I was going to say — physically present, mentally absent, as usual.
She smiled during the lecture but has no memory of it — physically present, mentally on Mars.
Trying to understand my professor with a thick accent and a broken mic — whisper challenge but it's life.
My mom gave me chores in vague terms — whisper challenge but it's life, and I failed.
I eat cereal with water when I'm out of milk — we listen and we don't judge, okay?
She still has her middle school diary — we listen and we don't judge, that's between her and God.
No because you're right, that shade of blue does look better on me — validation with extra steps.
He said my hair looks better curly — no because you're right, I'm switching styles permanently.
She finished her presentation and the whole class clapped — you ate and left no crumbs, queen.
His birthday speech was so good, everyone was emotional — you ate and left no crumbs, but also left us crying.
The chef made a five-course meal for 50 people — ate and left no crumbs, not even a breadstick survived.
Two plus two equals four — the math is mathing, no surprises today.
She budgeted $50 and spent exactly $50 — the math is mathing, and I'm shook.
He said he'd be late and arrived 30 minutes late — the math is mathing, unfortunately.
If I return this $20 shirt, I basically made $20, so buying this $40 dress is actually saving money — girl math in action.
Not going out tonight saves me $30, which means I can afford that $80 eyeshadow palette — girl math, don't question it.
He said buying a $60 video game saves money because it's hours of entertainment — boy math, I'm not convinced.
He spent $200 on new sneakers but won't buy new socks — boy math, priorities are questionable.
We've been debating toppings for 20 minutes — looks like we're gonna have to agree to agree on pineapple.
The group chat fought over plans for an hour — looks like we're gonna have to agree to agree on pizza.
He asked me to do his homework after he ignored my texts — the audacity is loud tonight, sir.
She showed up empty-handed to the potluck and ate three plates — the audacity is loud tonight, and it's not muted.
He put his cold feet on my legs under the blanket — he's a menace to society and my warmth.
She ate the last cookie and hid the evidence — she's a menace, but I forgive her because it was chocolate chip.
He wrote a poem for his girlfriend and recited it at a coffee shop — certified lover boy, very brave.
She made a scrapbook of every date they've been on — certified lover girl, goals honestly.
I cried at a couple's TikTok at 2 AM — hopeless romantic, no shame.
He still believes in handwritten letters — hopeless romantic, but also very cute.
He said 'let's see where it goes' for the tenth time — emotionally unavailable, next.
She listened to my feelings then changed the subject — emotionally unavailable, red flag.
I texted her 'hey' and she replied in 2 seconds and I panicked — attachment style: anxious, definitely.
He left me on read for three days — attachment style: avoidant, classic.
We communicated openly and resolved the issue — attachment style: secure, we're thriving.
I got an A on the exam and thought it was a mistake — imposter syndrome is my roommate.
She was offered a promotion and immediately doubted herself — imposter syndrome strikes again.
The mall was too loud, too bright, and too crowded — I'm overstimulated, time to leave.
Three notifications, a phone call, and someone talking to me at once — overstimulated, don't talk to me for an hour.
After the crowded bus, I'm touched out — don't breathe near me.
The toddler hugged me for ten minutes straight — touched out, need a sensory break.
Studied for finals all week — I'm burnt out, my brain is a desert.
She worked seven days straight and now can't form sentences — burnt out, send coffee.
I'm exhausted, but I finished my to-do list — burnt out but thriving, somehow.
She cried during lunch but aced her exam — burnt out but thriving, that's the hustle.
I want to be a CEO but I also don't want to leave my bed — lazy but with ambition, it's a struggle.
He wants to start a business but sleeps until noon — lazy but with ambition, the worst combo.
I'm choosing peace over drama, naps over parties — soft life era, welcome to the club.
She quit her stressful job for a cozy remote gig — soft life era, we love to see it.
Working 9 to 5 just to pay bills — the rat race is exhausting, I'm tapping out.
He's grinding every day but never happy — caught in the rat race, need a exit strategy.
My alarm didn't go off, I missed the bus, and I forgot my lunch — the universe is testing me today.
She said 'the universe is testing me' after her phone died mid-call — relatable energy.
I tried to buy coffee but the machine was broken — the universe is saying no to caffeine today.
Every store was out of my size — the universe is saying no to new clothes, I guess.
Found a parking spot right in front — the universe is saying yes for once.
She got the job she wanted without even applying — the universe is saying yes, loudly.
I'm manifesting good grades by staring at my textbook — it's not working, but I'm committed.
She wrote 'promotion' on her mirror every day — manifesting with confidence.
She asked for a raise and got it — manifesting queen, give her a crown.
He visualized free tickets and won a radio contest — manifesting king, teach me your ways.
Put on my headphones and walked around the block — hot girl walk for my mental health.
She does a hot girl walk every morning with an iced coffee in hand — iconic routine.
Winter hot girl walk requires a coat and resentment — still counts.
Rainy hot girl walk is just hot girl sprint to shelter — survival mode.
He said he saw a ghost in the library — that's crazy, I would have screamed.
She finished a 12-page essay in two hours — that's crazy, I need her powers.
He ate a whole pizza by himself — that's wild, but I'm not surprised.
She ran a marathon with no training — that's wild and dangerous, but okay.
He won the lottery twice — that's insane, I'm jealous.
She finished first in a race she didn't train for — that's insane, but we stan.
Everyone was arguing, and I just ate my snack — unbothered in my lane.
He ignored the rumors and kept doing his work — unbothered, very sigma.
She wore what she wanted despite the hate — unbothered queen, very demure.
He showed up in pajamas to the meeting — unbothered king, bold move.
I did my skincare, drank water, and avoided drama — unbothered, moisturized, thriving.
She posted a selfie after her ex texted — unbothered, moisturized, thriving, we love to see it.
I'm not checking my phone until noon — unbothered era, don't test me.
He's not responding to negativity — unbothered era, focusing on himself.
Failed my driving test twice — officially in my flop era, no license for me.
She tripped in front of her crush — flop era energy, but we've all been there.
I canceled plans without guilt — villain era, protecting my peace.
He told his boss he needs a raise or he's leaving — villain era, respect.
Bought myself flowers and journaled in a cafe — main character era, starring me.
She's traveling solo and posting aesthetic pics — main character era, the movie is beautiful.
Stopped working out and ate junk food for a month — glow down, but it was delicious.
He got a bad haircut before pictures — glow down, but hair grows back.
Joining a gym and a skincare routine — glow up incoming, watch this space.
She deleted social media to focus on herself — glow up incoming, the return will be epic.
She's a 10 but her phone case is a flip phone from 2004 — retro, I guess.
He's a 10 but his laugh sounds like a dying seal — subtract a few points.
He's a 10 but his bed is just a mattress on the floor — red flag.
I'm a 10 but I cry during every Pixar movie — emotional, not a flaw.
Snacks and leftover pasta for dinner — girl dinner, don't judge me.
A bowl of grapes and some cheese — girl dinner is whatever fits on a plate.
Buying coffee every day is cheaper than therapy — girl math, I'm sure of it.
If I don't buy anything today, I saved $20 — girl math, but I did buy lunch.
She ran a meeting and got coffee — girl boss energy, very busy.
He called himself a girl boss and we all laughed — not how that works.
I tried to bake cookies and burned water — girl failure, but I'll try again.
She tripped going up the stairs — girl failure moment, but she's still cool.
Your new haircut is giving 'I need a manager' — very Karen, but make it fashion.
This classroom is giving 'sad beige' — we need color.
His apology was giving 'I got caught' — not sincere.
It's the way he said 'actually' for me — condescending much?
It's the 7 AM alarm for me — we are not friends.
Not me crying over a tiktok about dogs — emotionally unstable.
Not me forgetting my own phone number — brain not braining.
Not me thinking today was Friday — wishful thinking.
Me after pulling an all-nighter: unresponsive zombie.
Me after eating spicy food: regrets and water.
Me when the teacher assigns group work: internal screaming.
Me when I see a dog: cannot function, must pet.
Staying up late to finish nothing — me fr.
Avoiding texts for no reason — me fr.
When he said 'I'm tired' — real, same.
She said she hates group projects — real, I felt that.
A meme about hating mornings — this is so real.
A tweet about forgetting why you walked into a room — this is so real.
I'll finish this assignment by tonight — on god, I promise.
On god, that was the best pizza I've ever had.
On god, if he texts me first I'll be shocked.
I deadass forgot my own middle name — embarrassed.
She deadass walked into a pole while on her phone — we saw it.
I'm not kidding, frfr — serious.
That movie was boring frfr — zero excitement.
I'm tired fr — need a nap.
She's so nice fr — genuine.
I passed the exam, nbs — no bs, I'm shocked too.
He apologized, nbs — actually sincere.
Ngl, I cried during that commercial — embarrassing but true.
Ngl, his outfit is better than mine — I'll admit it.
Ngl, I'm scared of bugs — irrational but real.
Tbh, I'd rather stay home — zero social battery.
Tbh, she was right — I was wrong.
Tbh, pineapple on pizza is fine — controversial maybe.
She aced the test — iktr, she studies hard.
He showed up early — iktr, responsible king.
If you know the smell of old books — iykyk.
The struggle of finding a parking spot — iykyk.
The pain of a dead phone with no charger — iykyk.
Age, school, location? — asl?
My asl is 20, college, ny — generic info.
Don't share your asl online — safety first.
Pov: you forgot your homework at home — panic.
Pov: you're the last one to get lunch — sad salad.
Pov: you're trying to be productive but it's 2 am — why am I like this.
Pov: you see a spider in your room — sleepover cancelled.
Fit check: hoodie, jeans, sneakers — basic.
She posted a fit check before leaving — confidence.
His fit check was just a suit — okay, formal.
Fit check for the gym: leggings and a tshirt — comfy.
Ootd: black on black — goth lite.
She posted her ootd with the caption 'slay' — period.
